Function: Core ACIS interface for single/multi-station climate summaries (temperature, precipitation, degree days).
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, ag extension agents, planners
Impact Evidence: Embedded in NE state climatologist workflows; referenced in Cornell extension guides
Notes: Primary ACIS data interface for the NRCC region; backbone for many downstream tools

Function: ACIS-based precipitation and temperature departure maps for monthly/state analysis.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, planners, media
Impact Evidence: Used in monthly state climate summaries distributed to NE planning agencies
Notes: Monthly/state map products; standard monitoring output; national ACIS maps exist but lack regional customization

Function: State and regional snow depth and snow water equivalent analyses.
User Segment: Water resource managers, hydrologists, planners, NWS forecasters
Notes: NRCS has national snow survey data but NRCC provides NE-specific analyses and reporting format

Function: Provides daily climate normals, max/min temperatures, precipitation, and degree days for forecast areas.
User Segment: NWS forecasters, researchers, general public
Notes: Canonical source is NOAA NWS NOWData; NRCC hosts regional landing page and context

Function: Portal describing how to access NRCC’s data (ACIS, CLIMOD, FTP requests).
User Segment: Researchers, developers, data analysts
Notes: Informational/navigation page; low standalone restoration value

Function: Regional monitoring, precipitation, and anomaly maps for NE states.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, state agencies, media
Impact Evidence: Referenced in NE state climate assessment documents
Notes: Main NRCC homepage monitoring suite; ACIS-dependent, recoverable if ACIS intact

Function: Climate summaries, graphics, and projections for each Northeast state. (hosted jointly with NCEI)
User Segment: Planners, policymakers, researchers, educators, media
Impact Evidence: Referenced in state hazard mitigation plans and climate adaptation frameworks across NE states
Notes: Joint NRCC/NCEI product; high policy visibility; NCEI has national version but NRCC provides NE depth

Function: PDF-based monthly/seasonal reports and summaries for states and region.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, historians
Notes: Static PDF reports; archivable via Wayback; low reconstruction priority

Function: Maps showing seasonal freeze risk for apple buds/blossoms by variety.
User Segment: Apple growers, orchard managers, ag extension agents, crop insurance adjusters
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Cornell Cooperative Extension apple production guides; used by NY apple industry
Notes: Unique NE-specific tool; no national substitute; high ag sector impact

Function: Provides bud hardiness temperatures for Concord, Riesling, Cabernet Franc, etc.
User Segment: Vineyard managers, viticulturalists, ag extension agents, crop insurance adjusters
Impact Evidence: Cited in Cornell viticulture extension publications; used by Finger Lakes and Hudson Valley wine industry
Notes: Unique regional tool for NE viticulture; no national equivalent at variety-level resolution

Function: Estimated lawn watering needs using evapotranspiration and rainfall data.
User Segment: Homeowners, grounds managers, municipal parks, extension agents
Notes: Recoverable via Wayback; moderate user base; some ET tools exist elsewhere

Function: Frost occurrence tracker for turf/grounds managers.
User Segment: Golf course managers, turf professionals, grounds managers
Notes: Niche but unique; no direct national equivalent for turf frost at regional scale

Function: Modeled GDD accumulation for mosquito life cycles and control timing.
User Segment: Public health agencies, vector control programs, county health departments
Impact Evidence: Used by NE county mosquito control programs prior to archival
Notes: Recoverable logic; public health application with no direct ACIS-based substitute in NE

Function: Modeled pest emergence timing based on regional GDD data.
User Segment: Ag extension agents, IPM specialists, farmers, crop consultants
Notes: NEWA (Network for Environment and Weather Applications) provides some NE pest forecasting; partial overlap

Function: Historic tool for computing cumulative degree days by station.
User Segment: Farmers, ag extension agents, crop consultants, agronomists
Impact Evidence: Historically embedded in NE extension crop management guides
Notes: CSF GDD Calculator is active partial substitute; original tool had broader station coverage

Function: Regional soil temperature and moisture conditions from ACIS + station data.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension, conservation districts
Notes: USDA has national-scale soil moisture; NE regional resolution and ACIS integration is not replicated

Tool Name: Cornell Climate Smart Farming (CSF) Decision Tools
Function: Collection of 9+ interactive tools supporting Northeast agriculture (listed below).
User Segment: NE farmers, ag extension agents, crop consultants, conservation districts, irrigation managers
Impact Evidence: Cited in USDA NRCS outreach; referenced in NY state agricultural adaptation planning documents
Notes: Flagship NRCC applied agriculture product; 9+ integrated tools; unique in NE; hosted on climatesmartfarming.org

Function: Computes accumulated GDDs for user-selected station and crop type.
User Segment: Farmers, crop consultants, ag extension agents
Impact Evidence: Included in Cornell Cooperative Extension crop management resources
Notes: Active; ACIS-backed; one of the most-used CSF tools

Function: Calculates soil water balance using weather and soil data.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension agents
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Cornell irrigation management extension publications
Notes: Unique NE tool; integrates ET and precipitation for field-scale water balance

Function: Identifies optimal cover crop planting windows for NE farmers.
User Segment: NE farmers, conservation districts, ag extension agents, NRCS staff
Impact Evidence: Referenced in NRCS cover crop planning materials for NE states
Notes: Unique operational decision support; no national equivalent at this specificity

Tool Name: CSF Apple Freeze Damage Probability Tool
Function: Combines bud stage models with forecast data to estimate freeze risk.
User Segment: Apple growers, orchard managers, ag extension, crop insurance adjusters
Impact Evidence: Used by NY State apple industry; cited in Cornell Extension integrated pest management resources
Notes: Most sophisticated apple frost tool in NE; integrates bud phenology with forecasts

Tool Name: CSF Grape Hardiness & Freeze Risk Tool
Function: Similar to NRCC’s grape tool but integrated into CSF portal.
User Segment: Vineyard managers, viticulturalists, ag extension agents
Impact Evidence: Used by Finger Lakes Wine Country and Hudson Valley wine associations
Notes: Integrated CSF version of grape hardiness tool; unique in NE

Function: Displays observed & projected climate trends since 1950 at the county level.
User Segment: Municipal planners, ag extension educators, researchers, journalists
Notes: NOAA Climate Explorer offers partial substitute; CSF version unique for NE county-level ag framing

Function: Calculates freezing/thawing index for roadbed engineering and load restrictions.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: DOT engineers, road maintenance crews, municipal public works
Impact Evidence: Referenced in NE state DOT pavement management guides
Notes: No direct national substitute at regional station resolution; relevant for load restriction decisions

Function: Provided historical data on icing frequency and surface temp anomalies for airports.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: Airport operations, airlines, FAA regional staff
Notes: NWS provides operational icing forecasts; historical NRCC tool offered climatological context

Function: Provided heating/cooling degree day summaries for building energy management.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: Building energy managers, utilities, engineers, HVAC contractors
Notes: NCEI provides HDD/CDD data nationally; NRCC tool offered station-level NE interface; moderate restoration value

Function: Summaries of daily snow accumulation and melt rates for plowing operations.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: Municipal DPW, road crews, grounds managers, snow contractors
Notes: No direct national substitute for operational snow management summaries at NE station level

Function: Soil temp index for golf/turf management scheduling.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: Golf course managers, turf professionals, sports field managers
Notes: Niche; USDA has some soil temp data; moderate restoration feasibility

Function: Calculated freeze potential based on soil/air temperature gradients.
Category: Infrastructure, Roads, Turf, and Urban Applications
User Segment: DOT engineers, municipalities, road maintenance crews
Notes: Complementary to Roadway Freezing/Thawing Index; no direct national substitute

Function: Generates Intensity–Duration–Frequency (IDF) curves and precipitation depth charts.
User Segment: Stormwater engineers, municipal planners, transportation engineers, FEMA floodplain managers
Impact Evidence: Embedded in NY stormwater design standards; cited in municipal engineering guidance documents
Notes: NOAA Atlas 14 exists but this tool provides user-friendly IDF curve generation; widely used in NE engineering practice

Function: Visualizes projected rainfall intensity changes under future climate scenarios for NY State.
User Segment: Stormwater engineers, municipal planners, climate adaptation consultants
Impact Evidence: Referenced in NYS DEC stormwater design guidance; used in NY climate resilience planning
Notes: Unique nationally; climate-adjusted IDF for NY; critical for infrastructure design under changing precipitation regimes

Function: Displays accumulated precipitation anomalies across Northeast states.
User Segment: Hydrologists, planners, emergency managers, researchers
Notes: Overlaps with ACIS map products; distinct precipitation frequency framing useful for flood planning

Function: Estimated daily/seasonal water balance using precip & ET data.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, water resource planners, conservation districts
Notes: CSF Water Deficit Calculator is partial substitute; original had broader agricultural and planning scope

Function: Tracked drought indices, streamflow percentile maps, and SPI values.
User Segment: Water resource managers, emergency managers, municipal water utilities, planners
Notes: Complex multi-source integration; USGS and NDMC have substitute components; NE-specific integration is the loss

Function: Repository of reports, assessments, and state summaries.
User Segment: Researchers, educators, planners, policymakers, journalists
Notes: Largely static repository; content can be archived; low reconstruction priority

Function: Shows observed & projected temperature/precip trends since 1950 at county scale.
User Segment: Planners, extension educators, researchers
Notes: Duplicate entry pointing to same CSF County Climate Change Tool as row 24

Function: Hosted K–12 climate learning modules and datasets (retired).
User Segment: K-12 educators, students
Notes: Many climate education resources exist nationally; low unique restoration value

Function: Early web-based climate visualization atlas for NE U.S.
User Segment: Researchers, educators, planners
Notes: Older web-based atlas; visualization framework likely deprecated; archival value over restoration

Tool Name: Regional Assessment Tools / Climate Futures Maps
Function: Visualization of projected changes in precipitation and temperature across NE.
User Segment: Planners, researchers, policymakers
Notes: NOAA Climate Explorer and Climate Central offer partial substitutes; NE-specific framing is the value

Function: Early version of CLIMOD2 interface for climate summaries.
User Segment: Researchers (historical reference only)
Notes: Superseded by CLIMOD2; archival interest only

Function: Historical drought monitoring tool.
User Segment: Farmers, drought monitors, ag extension
Notes: Drought monitoring function now served by NDMC and NOAA; ACIS-based logic recoverable

Function: Estimated ET from temperature, wind speed, and solar radiation.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, researchers
Notes: ET estimation available through other NE tools (NEWA, CSF); archival interest

Function: Estimated frost depth using ACIS soil and air temperature data.
User Segment: Road engineers, DOT, municipalities, building contractors
Notes: No direct substitute for ACIS-based NE soil frost depth; moderate niche value

Function: Interactive map for monthly climatologies (temperature, precip normals).
User Segment: Researchers, educators, planners
Notes: CLIMOD2 serves similar purpose; archival interest

Function: Provided historic severe weather plots by county; later absorbed into NCEI.
User Segment: Researchers, emergency managers, historians
Notes: Functionality absorbed into NCEI severe weather products; no restoration value
